result.summary: Adds input validation to the parser and reorders the cache eviction path so flush runs after unlink.
result.risk_surface: Reordered eviction may lose the entry if a crash lands between unlink and flush.

findings.title: Eviction unlinks the entry before persisting it
findings.explanation: evict() now unlinks the node before flush(), so a crash between the two loses the write.

findings.title: Empty input silently maps to None
findings.explanation: validate('') returns None and the caller proceeds without a value.

findings.title: strip() may return an empty string the caller treats as valid
findings.explanation: After stripping, an all-whitespace input becomes '' but is returned as-is.

findings.title: Stale docstring on evict()
findings.explanation: The docstring still describes flush-before-unlink ordering.

findings.title: Unused import textwrap
findings.explanation: textwrap is imported but never referenced.

findings.title: Eviction unlinks the entry before persisting it
Empty input silently maps to None
strip() may return an empty string the caller treats as valid

result.rationale: The added import line has no matching use in the diff.
The docstring is not visible in the diff hunks; cannot confirm from diff evidence alone.
The caller checks for falsy returns two lines below the hunk; '' is handled.
validate('') returns None and no caller in the diff guards it; escalating — the parse path dereferences the result.
The hunk moves flush(key) after _unlink(key); the loss window is real.

result.summary: 2 blockers (eviction loss window; empty-input escalation), 1 nit confirmed, 1 refuted, 1 needs a human look.
result.report_markdown:
## Review — request changes

### Blockers
- **COR-1** src/cache.py:40 — eviction unlinks before persisting; crash window loses the write.
- **COR-2** src/parser.py:88 — empty input maps to None and is dereferenced downstream (escalated).

### Needs human
- **COR-4** src/cache.py:38 — stale evict() docstring (not provable from the diff).

### Nits
- **COR-5** src/parser.py:1 — unused import textwrap.
